dominant_palette: black, silver, ivory/cream, gold, deep red/burgundy
composition_vocabulary: Tiles alternate between stark object-on-black isolation and dense layered arrangements. Central placement dominates — the hero object is framed symmetrically and given room, occasionally edge-bled for tension. Graphic-design tiles use negative space aggressively; photographic tiles lean into tight cropping that suggests weight and solidity.
lighting_quality: Predominantly chiaroscuro — deep shadows and controlled single-source or rim lighting that gives objects a sculptural, almost theatrical gravity; a minority of tiles use flat editorial-studio light.
material_textures: polished silver, oxidised metal, brushed gold, carved stone/bone, parchment/aged paper, smooth glass/resin
aesthetic_register: Mystic-talisman fused with streetwear-adjacent luxury — objects carry coded meaning and ceremony, but the graphic language and brand references (Trapstar, Tom Ford) ground it in contemporary cultural capital rather than antique heirloom.
notably_absent: Soft, feminine styling cues — florals, pearls, delicate chain arrangements, pastel editorial light, or the kind of lifestyle-aspirational context (wrist shots, flat-lays on linen) common on luxury jewellery moodboards are entirely absent.
